primary_factors: No snow/ice accumulation signal for the Tue 3/10 morning commute (temps well above freezing; precip type rain and mainly later)
Dense fog is the only meaningful operational hazard, with near-zero pockets possible around ~5–6 AM and reduced visibility continuing into 6–8 AM bus window
No confirmed ‘domino effect’ from neighboring district closures; official channels show no closure/delay posts as of the latest scan
Road infrastructure/plow operations are not a factor (roads expected clear/damp rather than snow-covered)
Power grid conditions look stable (low statewide outage counts; no evidence of a widespread outage event)
decision_rationale: All six inputs align on a non-snow-day setup for Rockford, MI on Tue 2026-03-10: temperatures remain well above freezing during the hours that matter, and there is no accumulating snow or freezing rain indicated for the morning commute. The only credible disruption mechanism is dense fog with reduced visibility during the bus window (6–8 AM), including the potential for brief near-zero pockets around 5–6 AM in low-lying/rural areas. Historically, fog alone rarely drives a full district closure in West Michigan; it more commonly results in normal operations with heightened transportation precautions, and occasionally a 2-hour delay if widespread visibility is persistently very low right at decision time. News/community signals do not show neighboring district closures, official “monitoring” posts, or event-cancellation buzz, and infrastructure/power do not indicate additional compounding risks. The safety analyst clarified the earlier ‘moderate’ ice risk is mainly a Tue night → Wed concern, not a Tue AM driver. Therefore: very low closure probability, with a non-trivial (but still minority) chance of a delay if visibility verifies worse than forecast across the entire district at 6:00–7:00 AM.

collaboration.summary: Agents reached consensus after 1 round(s) with 2.0% spread. Average probability: 3%.
